Description: Validating external input reduces the risk of injection and other attacks.
Limiting privileges reduces the impact of compromised accounts or components.
Proper authorization enforces who can perform which actions.
Audit logs help detect and investigate security incidents.
Proper secrets management prevents credential leakage.
Input validation and parameterized queries mitigate injection risks.
Secure defaults discourage unsafe deserialization options.
Security fixes around injection vulnerabilities are high-risk in legacy systems.
